verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a sperm-specific gamete fusion protein with a single-pass TM domain and Ig-like extracellular architecture. It is surface-displayed on sperm to enable plasma-membrane binding and fusion with the oocyte during fertilization. However, this surface display is restricted to the male germline (sperm) and occurs only during the narrow developmental window of spermatogenesis and gamete interaction. While the protein possesses its own membrane-anchor mechanism (TM domain with extracellular domain), its expression and surface presentation are confined to a specialized, developmentally-restricted cell lineage (spermatogenic cells / mature sperm). This qualifies as tissue-restricted surface display rather than baseline somatic surface accessibility.
